questions.question: What is the difference between @NotNull and @NotBlank?
options: @NotNull only works on String; @NotBlank works on any type
@NotNull checks that the value is not null; @NotBlank checks not null AND not empty/whitespace (String only)
@NotBlank is the newer version that replaces @NotNull in Java 17+
They are identical — @NotBlank is just an alias for @NotNull

questions.question: Where must @Valid be placed to trigger Bean Validation on an incoming request body?
options: On the DTO record class definition
On the @RequestBody parameter in the controller method signature
On the @Service method that processes the DTO
On the @PostMapping annotation

questions.question: To validate a path variable like @GetMapping('/{id}') with @Min(1), what must be added to the controller class?
options: @EnableValidation
@Valid on the class
@Validated on the controller class
Nothing — @Min automatically works on @PathVariable

questions.question: When Spring throws MethodArgumentNotValidException, what HTTP status does the default Spring error handler return?
options: 200 OK
400 Bad Request
422 Unprocessable Entity
500 Internal Server Error

questions.question: In a custom ConstraintValidator, what should isValid() return when the field value is null?
options: false — null values are always invalid
true — let dedicated null-check annotations (@NotNull, @NotBlank) handle null
It should throw a NullPointerException
It depends on the annotation's require attribute
